blueprint.title: Commercial Bank AI Demo: Fraud Triage Automation & SMB Loan Underwriting Intelligence

personas.name: Fraud Operations Analyst at a mid-market commercial bank
personas.description: Reviews and dispositions fraud alerts across debit, credit, and ACH channels daily, managing a queue of 300+ rules-based alerts with a false-positive rate exceeding 85%.
goals: Reduce time spent on false-positive alert review from hours to minutes
Prioritize genuinely suspicious transactions for escalation before customer impact
Maintain audit-ready documentation for every alert disposition
pain_points: Rules-based system generates overwhelming alert volume with minimal contextual scoring
Cross-channel correlation requires manual lookups across three separate systems
High false-positive rate erodes analyst confidence and causes alert fatigue

personas.name: SMB Credit Underwriter at a mid-market commercial bank
personas.description: Underwrites small business loans between $50K and $2M, manually spreading bank statements and tax returns to assess cash-flow health and creditworthiness.
goals: Normalize and spread 24 months of bank statements and two years of tax returns within one business day
Surface cash-flow discrepancies and revenue inconsistencies before credit committee review
Produce a defensible credit memo with quantified risk factors
pain_points: Manual data extraction from PDFs and scanned documents takes 4-6 hours per application
Inconsistent spreading templates across underwriters create audit risk
Discrepancies between stated revenue on tax returns and actual deposits are often missed until late in the process

personas.name: BSA/AML Compliance Officer at a mid-market commercial bank
personas.description: Oversees fraud escalation workflows and ensures that AI-assisted decisions meet regulatory documentation standards under BSA, Reg E, and NACHA rules.
goals: Ensure every AI-assisted fraud disposition has a human-reviewable audit trail
Validate that underwriting outputs comply with fair lending and documentation requirements
Monitor model drift and alert threshold performance on a monthly basis
pain_points: Lack of explainability in current rules engine makes regulatory examination responses difficult
No centralized audit log linking alert disposition to analyst action and supporting evidence
Underwriting process inconsistencies create fair lending exposure during HMDA reviews

scenarios.title: Cross-Channel Fraud Triage: ACH Batch Anomaly Linked to Debit Card Compromise
scenarios.description: An overnight ACH batch triggers 47 alerts. The fraud analyst must determine which alerts represent genuine account takeover activity versus legitimate payroll runs, correlating signals across ACH, debit, and credit channels before the morning settlement window closes.
steps: Fraud Operations Analyst opens the morning alert queue; the Alert Prioritization Agent has already scored and ranked all 47 ACH alerts by risk tier, suppressing 31 as low-risk based on historical payroll patterns and counterparty reputation.

Analyst reviews the 16 elevated-risk alerts; the Cross-Channel Correlation Agent surfaces that 9 of the flagged accounts also had card-not-present debit transactions in a foreign jurisdiction within the prior 48 hours.

For the 9 correlated accounts, the Fraud Evidence Synthesis Agent assembles a case package including transaction timelines, device fingerprint changes, and velocity metrics, and recommends account suspension with a confidence score and plain-language rationale.

Analyst reviews the synthesized case packages, confirms 8 of 9 as true positives, overrides 1, and submits dispositions; all actions are logged by the Compliance Audit Agent with analyst ID, timestamp, and evidence snapshot.

BSA/AML Compliance Officer reviews the shift summary report generated by the Compliance Audit Agent, confirming that all dispositions include required Reg E documentation fields and escalation rationale.

scenarios.title: SMB Loan Underwriting: Cash-Flow Discrepancy Detection for a $750K Working Capital Line
scenarios.description: A regional HVAC contractor applies for a $750K working capital line of credit. The underwriter must spread 24 months of business bank statements and two years of Schedule C and corporate tax returns, then reconcile reported revenue against actual deposit activity before the credit committee meeting.
steps: SMB Credit Underwriter uploads 26 PDF documents (bank statements and tax returns) to the loan origination portal; the Document Ingestion and Normalization Agent parses, classifies, and extracts structured data from all documents within 12 minutes, flagging 3 pages with low OCR confidence for manual review.

The Cash-Flow Spreading Agent constructs a 24-month cash-flow model, calculating average monthly deposits, operating expense ratios, debt service coverage, and seasonal revenue patterns across the two bank accounts on file.

The Discrepancy Detection Agent compares Schedule C gross receipts ($1.82M reported) against total annual deposits across both accounts ($1.54M deposited), flags a $280K gap, and identifies two months where large round-number transfers between accounts inflated apparent revenue.

The underwriter reviews the discrepancy report, adds a narrative note explaining the intercompany transfer pattern after a call with the borrower, and adjusts the spreading model; the Credit Memo Drafting Agent generates a structured credit memo with risk ratings, adjusted DSCR, and the underwriter's annotated findings.

BSA/AML Compliance Officer spot-checks the credit memo for fair lending compliance, confirms the AI-generated spreading model matches the bank's standard template, and approves the package for credit committee submission.

agents.name: Alert Prioritization Agent
agents.role: Scores and ranks inbound fraud alerts across debit, credit, and ACH channels using behavioral baselines and counterparty intelligence to suppress low-risk noise before analyst review.
responsibilities: Ingest raw alert feeds from the core banking rules engine across all three payment channels
Score each alert against account behavioral baselines, counterparty reputation databases, and historical false-positive patterns
Assign a risk tier (Critical, Elevated, Low) and estimated false-positive probability to each alert
Suppress low-risk alerts with documented rationale and route elevated alerts to the analyst queue
inputs: Real-time and batch alert payloads from the rules-based fraud detection system
12-month account transaction history per flagged account
Counterparty ACH originator reputation scores
Historical analyst disposition outcomes for model feedback
outputs: Ranked alert queue with risk tier labels and false-positive probability scores
Suppression log with per-alert rationale for compliance review
Daily alert volume and suppression rate summary for operations management

agents.name: Cross-Channel Correlation Agent
agents.role: Links fraud signals across debit, credit, and ACH channels for the same account or customer entity to surface multi-vector attack patterns invisible to single-channel rules.
responsibilities: Query debit, credit, and ACH transaction histories for all accounts appearing in elevated-risk alerts
Identify temporal and behavioral correlations such as credential changes, geographic anomalies, and velocity spikes across channels
Cluster related accounts when shared device fingerprints, IP addresses, or beneficiary routing numbers suggest coordinated fraud rings
Deliver a correlation summary to the analyst for each flagged account cluster
inputs: Elevated-risk alert list from the Alert Prioritization Agent
Full transaction history across debit, credit, and ACH systems for flagged accounts
Device fingerprint and IP geolocation logs from digital banking platform
Beneficiary and originator routing number registry
outputs: Cross-channel correlation report per account or account cluster
Confidence-scored linkage map showing shared fraud indicators across channels
Recommended escalation groupings for analyst review

agents.name: Fraud Evidence Synthesis Agent
agents.role: Assembles structured case packages for confirmed high-risk alerts, combining transaction evidence, behavioral context, and a plain-language disposition recommendation for analyst decision-making.
responsibilities: Compile transaction timelines, velocity metrics, and anomaly descriptions for each high-risk account
Generate a plain-language summary of why the alert is suspicious, referencing specific data points
Produce a recommended disposition (suspend, monitor, clear) with a confidence score and supporting rationale
Format the case package to meet Reg E and NACHA documentation requirements
inputs: Correlation reports from the Cross-Channel Correlation Agent
Raw transaction records and account metadata for flagged accounts
Regulatory documentation templates for Reg E and NACHA dispute records
outputs: Structured case package per high-risk account including evidence timeline and disposition recommendation
Confidence score with factor breakdown for analyst transparency
Pre-populated Reg E dispute documentation fields ready for analyst review and submission

agents.name: Document Ingestion and Normalization Agent
agents.role: Parses, classifies, and extracts structured financial data from uploaded bank statements and tax return PDFs, handling varied formats and flagging low-confidence extractions for human review.
responsibilities: Classify uploaded documents by type (bank statement, Schedule C, Form 1120-S, etc.) and tax year or statement period
Extract structured fields including monthly deposits, withdrawals, beginning and ending balances, gross receipts, and deductions
Normalize data into a standardized spreading schema regardless of source bank or tax form version
Flag pages or fields with OCR confidence below threshold and queue them for underwriter manual review
inputs: PDF and image uploads from the loan origination portal
Bank statement format library covering major commercial banks
IRS tax form field mapping schemas for Schedule C, 1120, and 1120-S
outputs: Structured JSON data extract per document with field-level confidence scores
Document classification manifest listing each file, type, and coverage period
Low-confidence flag report listing specific pages and fields requiring manual verification

agents.name: Cash-Flow Spreading and Discrepancy Detection Agent
agents.role: Constructs a standardized 24-month cash-flow model from normalized financial data and identifies material discrepancies between reported income and actual deposit activity.
responsibilities: Build a month-by-month cash-flow spread including gross deposits, operating expenses, owner draws, debt service, and net cash position
Calculate key credit metrics including average monthly net cash flow, debt service coverage ratio, and revenue seasonality index
Compare tax return gross receipts against total bank deposit activity for the same periods and quantify any gap
Detect intercompany transfers, round-number anomalies, and deposit timing patterns that may inflate apparent revenue
inputs: Structured financial data from the Document Ingestion and Normalization Agent
Bank's standard credit spreading template and DSCR calculation methodology
Loan application data including stated revenue, existing debt obligations, and requested facility terms
outputs: 24-month cash-flow spread in the bank's standard template format
Calculated DSCR, average monthly net cash flow, and seasonality metrics
Discrepancy report quantifying gaps between reported and deposited revenue with flagged anomalous transactions

agents.name: Compliance Audit and Credit Memo Drafting Agent
agents.role: Generates audit-ready documentation for both fraud dispositions and underwriting decisions, ensuring outputs meet regulatory standards and are traceable to specific data inputs and analyst actions.
responsibilities: Log every fraud alert disposition with analyst ID, timestamp, AI recommendation, analyst decision, and evidence snapshot
Generate structured credit memos incorporating cash-flow spreads, discrepancy findings, risk ratings, and underwriter narrative annotations
Validate that fraud case packages include all required Reg E and NACHA fields before submission
Produce shift-level and monthly summary reports on alert disposition rates, suppression accuracy, and underwriting throughput for compliance officer review
inputs: Analyst disposition actions and override notes from the fraud triage workflow
Cash-flow spreads and discrepancy reports from the Cash-Flow Spreading and Discrepancy Detection Agent
Underwriter narrative annotations and adjusted model inputs
Regulatory documentation requirements for Reg E, NACHA, BSA, and fair lending
outputs: Immutable fraud disposition audit log entries per alert with full evidence linkage
Structured credit memo document ready for credit committee submission
Compliance validation report confirming required fields are present in fraud and underwriting packages
Monthly operations dashboard summarizing AI performance metrics for BSA/AML Compliance Officer review
